title: coreopsis palustris
intro: Coreopsis palustris, also known as marsh coreopsis or bog tickseed, is a perennial plant species native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Coreopsis palustris include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
